request.system:
You extract keyed dungeon areas from tabletop adventure module pages. The user message names the areas to extract, then interleaves each page's extracted text (each headed by a [page N] marker) with that page's image. Return one entry per named area, using exactly the canonical keys given.

Rules:
- "encounters" are the area's hostile or monstrous inhabitants with stat blocks. Ordinary townsfolk and named NPCs without a creature type are not encounters — they belong in the description. Use the creature type as printed ("goblin"), putting proper names ("Snagg") in the description. Wandering-monster tables are not keyed encounters.
- Encounter counts: put a plain number in "count_fixed" and dice notation like "2d4" in "count_dice"; anything the module says about count that fits neither (rates, conditions, ranges) goes in "count_note". Leave unused count fields null.
- "trap" describes the area's trap, null when it has none. "features" lists notable fixtures a referee should know about (altars, pools, levers, secret doors); "treasure" lists the area's treasure as printed.
- "connections": derive each connection's "direction" from the level map when the text is silent; use "unknown" only when neither the text nor the map says. For "to_key", use the connected area's canonical key when it appears in the area list; otherwise its printed label.
- "source_pages" refer to the [page N] markers in this request, never to page numbers printed on the pages.
- "confidence" is your self-assessment in [0, 1] of how faithfully you extracted that area.

parts.text:
Extract the keyed areas listed below from dungeon "I. Bugbear Lair" (id: i-bugbear-lair), level 1.
Areas to extract (canonical key | printed key | name | expected pages):
- 58 | printed key "58" | Entrance | pages 34
- 59 | printed key "59" | Swinging Arm Trap | pages 34
- 60 | printed key "60" | Store Room | pages 34
- 61 | printed key "61" | Common Room | pages 34
- 62 | printed key "62" | Empty Common Room | pages 34
- 63 | printed key "63" | Secret Door | pages 35
- 64 | printed key "64" | Treasure Room | pages 35
- 65 | printed key "65" | Prison | pages 35
- 66 | printed key "66" | Common Room | pages 35
- 67 | printed key "67" | Owlbear | pages 35
- 68 | printed key "68" | Main Boss | pages 35
This level's map is on page 38.

[page 34]
The Caves The Chaotic Caves
55. COMMON ROOM: Three males are busy
here, readying simple bamboo gigs for a fishand-frog hunt.
3 Lizard Men: AC 12, HD 2, #At 1 spear, 
Dam 1d6+1, Mv 30', Sv F2, ML 11, XP 75 ea.
HP 8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐ 8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
56. GUARD POST: Three lizard men (wearing
tough hide and bone armor) and a giant gecko
lizard on a thick fiber rope lurk in the dark
here, keeping intruders (including animals) out.
The tunnel slopes down sharply from the
opening to here, dropping some 30'. There are
several boulders here which can be rolled into
the hallway and packed with mud to prevent
flooding.
3 Lizard Men: AC 15, HD 2, #At 1 spear, 
Dam 1d6+1, Mv 20', Sv F2, ML 11, XP 75 ea.
HP 8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐ 8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
Giant Gecko Lizard: AC 15, HD 3+1, #At 1 bite, 
Dam 1d8, Mv 40', Sv F2, ML 7, XP 145
HP 13 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
57. SOUTH ENTRANCE: This entrance opens
into the bank of the stream, which is some 20
feet high here. It is flanked by two 9-foot
totem poles (featuring crude animals and
lizards) capped by bear skulls and feathers.
I. BUGBEAR LAIR
These bugbears are planning a takeover of the
region. For now they are biding their time and
waiting for an opportunity. Heavy wooden
beams support the ceilings of the halls of their
lair, tunneled from the loose, rocky earth by
skilled hands. Most ceilings are 15 feet high.
58. ENTRANCE: Two bugbears with pole arms
stand outside the entranceway, one on each
side. Another is in the niche inside. Each
carries 1d6 gp.
3 Bugbears: AC 15, HD 3+1, #At 1 polearm, 
Dam 1d10+1, Mv 30', Sv F3, ML 9, XP 145 ea.
HP 13 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
13 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
13 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
59. SWINGING ARM TRAP: Both ends of this
hall are protected by a trap. A swinging frame
set with sharp, hard wooden spikes slashes out
when a pressure plate is stepped on, attacking
as a 2 HD monster and doing 2d6 points of
damage to each person hit. Each person in
front might be hit.
60. STORE ROOM: The door to this room is
open. Just inside is a tarp hanging down to
prevent light from getting in or out, or so it
might seem. The tarp has been carefully
cultured with a yellow mold on the west side
such that anyone pushing brusquely through
will cause it to release spores. The bugbears
typically use a pole to carefully lift the tarp
aside from a distance. The room contains
crates of food, bags of meat, three 50-foot
sections of rope, a dozen iron spikes and other
items.
Yellow Mold: AC Can always be hit, HD 2*, 
#At Spores, Dam All within 10 feet of the mold 
will be affected by the spores and must save 
vs. Death Ray or take 1d8 points of damage per
round for 6 rounds, Mv 0, Sv NM, ML N/A, 
XP 100
HP 8 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
61. COMMON ROOM: Three bugbears reside
here. Each has 1d6 gp on its person. The east
wall is decorated with three long strips of
cheap paper scattered with bugbear "chickentracks" (writing). They are prayer scrolls
pleading for success in battle and many
enemies to feast on. 
3 Bugbears: AC 15, HD 3+1, #At 1 scimitar, 
Dam 1d8+1, Mv 30', Sv F3, ML 9, XP 145 ea.
HP 13 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
13 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
13 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
62. EMPTY COMMON ROOM: This room has
benches and tables stacked on the north wall
and bunks and boxes on the south wall. It is
empty.
32

[page 35]
The Chaotic Caves The Caves
63. SECRET DOOR: This secret door is well
hidden; it only opens when a block in the
ceiling overhead is gripped and pulled down (it
swings up; the latch is reset when it is swung
down).
The prison and main treasure room are hidden
to help throw off would-be rescuers or
plunderers. Captives are blindfolded before
entering or exiting this area to keep it secret.
64. TREASURE ROOM: The door is extremely
strong and locked by a high-quality lock (the
chief in room #68 has the key). Tucked away
on one side of the door frame is a small niche;
unless someone pokes a dagger blade or such
inside and presses on a latch there, a bladder
over the door releases a blast of gas that
floods the area outside the door; it catches fire
if open flame (torch, lantern, etc.) is nearby,
doing 1d6 points of damage to all within 10 feet
(half damage with save vs. Dragon Breath). If
there is no flame, it is harmless and dissipates
in 1d6 turns.
The room contains chests, boxes, and barrels.
Inside one chest is a Shield +1, a Potion of
Gaseous Form, and a Potion of Healing. A bag
holds 500 gp, 1,400 ep, and 200 cp.
65. PRISON: The head jailer is usually in and
around "e", along with an assistant. Each has
3d6 gp on his person. The heavy wooden doors
to cells "a" to "d" are locked, with barred
windows to allow someone to look inside
them. Area "e" is a torture room furnished
with stools for guards, and iron loops and
chains on the walls for victims. A hot brazier
and fireplace poker is ready at all times.
2 Bugbears: AC 13, HD 3+1, #At 1 scimitar, 
Dam 1d8+1, Mv 30', Sv F3, ML 9, XP 145 ea.
HP 13 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
13 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
Cells "a" and "d" are empty. "b" holds a
desperate merchant who will pay 200 gp in
thanks if returned to town. "c" has two
completely harmless skeletons of humans or
elves. 
66. COMMON ROOM: Three bugbears reside
here. Each has 1d6 gp on its person. The room
is furnished with common rattan furniture
(tables and chairs) and heavy wooden bunks.
3 Bugbears: AC 15, HD 3+1, #At 1 scimitar, 
Dam 1d8+1, Mv 30', Sv F3, ML 9, XP 145 ea.
HP 13 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
13 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
13 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
67. OWLBEAR: Listening to this door will reveal
a grunting, snuffling noise. Chained to the east
wall inside is a great owlbear, captured as a
cub and raised by the bugbears. It considers
the lair its home, and defends it ferociously
against intruders. The chain is weakly
attached to the wall, and the beast can easily
rip it out if it needs to. On occasion it is
allowed to roam free. It is only chained up to
keep it from getting into supplies and being
annoying. The bugbears often take it outside
with them.
Owlbear: AC 15, HD 5, #At 2 claws, 1 bite + 
1 hug, Dam 1d8 claw, 1d8 bite, 2d8 hug, Mv 40', 
Sv F5, ML 9, XP 360
HP 22 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐
☐☐
68. MAIN BOSS: The head bugbear and a small
guard of common bugbears reside here, amid
heavy wooden beds, chairs, and tables. The
chief has a necklace with 300 gp on it; each
individual has 1d6 gp on its person. The chief
has two keys on a thong around one wrist: one
opens the locked door to the west and the
other the treasure room (#64). The locked
door to the west is stout, thick, and ironbound, but the room it opens into is empty.
Bugbear Chief: AC 15, HD 6+6, #At 1 scimitar, 
Dam 1d8+3, Mv 30', Sv F6, ML 10, XP 500
HP 33 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐
☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
2 Bugbears: AC 15, HD 3+1, #At 1 polearm, 
Dam 1d10+1, Mv 30', Sv F3, ML 10 (9), XP 145 ea.
HP 13 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
13 ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐☐☐ ☐☐☐
33

areas.description: Entrance to the bugbear lair. Two bugbears with pole arms stand outside the entranceway, one on each side, and another waits in the niche just inside. Heavy wooden beams support the ceilings of the lair; most ceilings are 15 feet high.

Both ends of this hall are protected by the same type of mechanism: a swinging frame set with sharp, hard wooden spikes that slashes out when a pressure plate is stepped on. Each person in front might be hit.
areas.trap: Pressure plates at both ends trigger a swinging spiked wooden frame that attacks as a 2 HD monster and deals 2d6 damage to each person hit.

areas.description: The door to this room is open. Just inside hangs a tarp meant to prevent light from getting in or out. The west side of the tarp has been carefully cultured with yellow mold so that anyone pushing brusquely through it will release spores. The bugbears normally use a pole to lift the tarp aside from a distance. The room contains crates of food, bags of meat, three 50-foot sections of rope, a dozen iron spikes, and other items.

encounters.count_note: Cultured on the west side of the hanging tarp just inside the door.

areas.trap: A hanging tarp just inside the open door is cultured with yellow mold on its west side; pushing through carelessly releases spores affecting all within 10 feet.
features: Hanging tarp just inside the doorway

areas.description: Three bugbears reside here. The east wall is decorated with three long strips of cheap paper covered in bugbear "chicken-tracks" writing: prayer scrolls pleading for success in battle and many enemies to feast on.

encounters.count_note: Each has 1d6 gp on its person.

treasure: Each bugbear has 1d6 gp on its person.
features: Prayer scrolls on the east wall
Three long strips of cheap paper with bugbear writing

areas.description: This room has benches and tables stacked on the north wall and bunks and boxes on the south wall. It is empty.
features: Benches stacked on the north wall
Tables stacked on the north wall

areas.description: This secret door is well hidden. It opens only when a block in the ceiling overhead is gripped and pulled down; the door swings up, and the latch is reset when it is swung down. The prison and main treasure room are hidden beyond to foil rescuers or plunderers. Captives are blindfolded before entering or exiting this area to keep it secret.

The door to this room is extremely strong and locked with a high-quality lock; the chief in room 68 has the key. Chests, boxes, and barrels fill the room. A small niche is tucked away on one side of the door frame; unless a blade or similar object is inserted to press the hidden latch, a bladder over the door releases gas into the area outside the door.
areas.trap: A hidden latch in a small niche beside the door frame must be pressed to prevent a gas bladder over the door from releasing gas outside the room; if open flame is nearby, the gas ignites and deals 1d6 damage to all within 10 feet (save vs. Dragon Breath for half). Without flame it is harmless and dissipates in 1d6 turns.
features: Small niche hidden in the door frame

areas.description: The prison contains cells a through d and torture room e. The head jailer is usually in and around e with an assistant. The heavy wooden doors to cells a to d are locked and have barred windows for inspection. Area e has stools for guards, iron loops and chains on the walls for victims, and a hot brazier with fireplace poker ready at all times. Cells a and d are empty. Cell b holds a desperate merchant who will pay 200 gp in thanks if returned to town. Cell c contains two completely harmless skeletons of humans or elves.

encounters.count_note: Head jailer and assistant; each has 3d6 gp on his person.

treasure: Each jailer has 3d6 gp on his person.
A rescued merchant in cell b will pay 200 gp in thanks if returned to town.
features: Iron loops and chains on the walls
Cell b holds a desperate merchant
Cell c holds two harmless skeletons

areas.description: Three bugbears reside here. The room is furnished with common rattan furniture, including tables and chairs, and heavy wooden bunks.

encounters.count_note: Each has 1d6 gp on its person.

treasure: Each bugbear has 1d6 gp on its person.

areas.description: Listening at this door reveals grunting and snuffling. Inside, a great owlbear is chained to the east wall. It was captured as a cub and raised by the bugbears, considers the lair its home, and defends it ferociously against intruders. The chain is weakly attached to the wall and can easily be ripped out if needed. The bugbears sometimes let it roam free and often take it outside with them.
features: Door from which grunting and snuffling can be heard
Weak wall attachment for the chain

areas.description: The head bugbear and a small guard of common bugbears reside here among heavy wooden beds, chairs, and tables. The chief wears two keys on a thong around one wrist: one opens the locked door to the west and the other opens the treasure room (64). The locked western door is stout, thick, and iron-bound, but the room beyond it is empty.

encounters.count_note: Wears a necklace with 300 gp and carries two keys on a thong around one wrist.
Each individual has 1d6 gp on its person.

treasure: Each individual has 1d6 gp on its person.
Key to the locked western door
features: Stout thick iron-bound locked door to the west
Empty room beyond the western door
